-module(solver).
-export([solve/1]).

%% Helper function to check if arr is a cyclic shift of target.
%% arr and target must have the same length.
is_cyclic_shift(Arr, Target) ->
    case Arr of
        [] -> true;
        _ ->
            Len = length(Arr),
            DoubleTarget = [T || T <- Target, T <- Target],
            % Check all rotations of Target against Arr
            true = foldr(check_rotation, false, lists:seq(0, Len - 1)),
            where
                check_rotation(Start, Found) ->
                    if Found =:= true -> true;
                    true ->
                        % Generate the rotated target starting at 'Start'
                        RotatedTarget = lists:sublist(DoubleTarget, Start, Len),
                        % Compare Arr with RotatedTarget
                        same_list(Arr, RotatedTarget)
                    end.

%% Helper to check if two lists are identical
same_list(L1, L2) ->
    length(L1) =:= length(L2),
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, E1, E2) ->
        case Acc of
            true -> E1 =:= E2;
            false -> false
        end
    end, true, L1, L2).

%% Helper function to get all divisors of N
get_divisors(N) ->
    Divisors = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, K) ->
        case mod(N, K) of
            0 ->
                Rest = N div K,
                if K =:= Rest -> [K] ++ Acc;
                true -> [K, Rest] ++ Acc
                end;
            _ -> Acc
        end
    end, [], lists:seq(1, floor(sqrt(N)))).

%% Helper function to slice a list (equivalent to Python's slice)
%% sublist(List, StartIndex, Length)
lists:sublist(List, StartIndex, Length) ->
    lists:sublist(List, StartIndex, Length).

%% Main solving function
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),

    Divisors = get_divisors(N),
    
    SumOfK = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, K) ->
        if check_k(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->
            Acc + K;
        true -> Acc
        end
    end, 0, Divisors).

%% Checks if a given divisor K allows sorting the array Nums.
check_k(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->
    NumBlocks = N div K,
    
    % Check each block
    lists:foldl(fun(AllSortable, I) ->
        case AllSortable of
            false -> false; % Early exit if already found unsortable
            true ->
                Start = I * K,
                End = Start + K,
                
                % Extract current block
                Block = lists:sublist(Nums, Start, K),
                
                % Extract target block from sorted array
                TargetBlock = lists:sublist(SortedNums, Start, K),
                
                % Check if Block is a cyclic shift of TargetBlock
                is_cyclic_shift(Block, TargetBlock)
        end
    end, true, lists:seq(0, NumBlocks - 1)).

```erlang
-module(solver).

-export([solve/1]).

%% Helper function to check if arr is a cyclic shift of target
%% This is equivalent to checking if arr is a contiguous subarray of target ++ target.
is_cyclic_shift(Arr, Target) when is_list(Arr), is_list(Target) ->
    Length = length(Target),
    if Length == 0 ->
        true;
    true ->
        DoubleTarget = Target ++ Target,
        Len = length(Arr),
        
        % Check all possible starting positions in DoubleTarget
        lists:any(fun(Start) ->
            lists:equal(Arr, lists:sublist(DoubleTarget, Start, Len))
        end, lists:seq(1, Length))
        end.

%% Checks if a given divisor k allows sorting the array nums.
check_k(N, SortedNums, Nums, K) when is_integer(N), is_list(SortedNums), is_list(Nums), is_integer(K) ->
    if K == 0 orelse N rem K /= 0 ->
        false;
    true ->
        NumBlocks = N div K,
        
        lists:all(fun(BlockIdx) ->
            Start = BlockIdx * K,
            End = Start + K,
            
            % Extract block from Nums
            Block = lists:sublist(Nums, Start, K),
            
            % Extract corresponding target block from SortedNums
            TargetBlock = lists:sublist(SortedNums, Start, K),
            
            % Check if this block can be cyclically shifted to match its sorted target
            is_cyclic_shift(Block, TargetBlock)
        end, lists:seq(1, NumBlocks))
        end.

%% Main solver function
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % 1. Get the sorted version of nums
    SortedNums = erlang:sort(Nums),
    
    % 2. Find all divisors of N
    Divisors = find_divisors(N),
    
    % 3. Check each divisor k
    SumOfK = lists:sum([
        K || K <- Divisors, 
        check_k(N, SortedNums, Nums, K)
    ]),
    
    SumOfK.

%% Finds all divisors of an integer n
find_divisors(N) when is_integer(N) ->
    Divs = [
        K || K <- lists:seq(1, floor(sqrt(N))),
        N rem K == 0,
        K
    ],
    
    Divs_complement = [
        N div K || K <- Divs, 
        N div K /= K
    ],
    
    lists:sort(Divs ++ Divs_complement).

%% Helper for lists:sublist(List, StartIndex, Length)
lists:sublist(List, Start, Length) ->
    lists:nth(Start + 1, List, Length).

%% Helper for lists:seq(Start, End)
lists:seq(Start, End) ->
    lists:seq_impl(Start, End).

lists:seq_impl(Start, End) when Start > End ->
    []
lists:seq_impl(Start, End) ->
    [Start | lists:seq_impl(Start + 1, End)].

%% Helper for lists:all(Predicate, List)
lists:all(Predicate, List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(X, Acc) ->
        if Predicate(X) == true -> Acc; true -> false
    end, true, List).

-module(solution_module).

% Helper function to check if one list is a cyclic shift of another.
% Assumes both lists have the same length and contain the same elements (multiset equality).
is_cyclic_shift(ListA, ListB) ->
    case {ListA, ListB} of
        {[], []} -> true;
        _ ->
            case get_length(ListA) of
                Length when Length > 0 ->
                    % Concatenate ListB with itself
                    DoubleListB = ListB ++ ListB,
                    % Check if ListA is a sublist of DoubleListB of length Length
                    sublist_of(Length, ListA, DoubleListB)
                _ -> false
            end
    end.

% Helper function to check if 'Sub' is a sublist of 'Main' of a specific length 'Len'.
sublist_of(Len, Sub, Main) when len(Sub) =:= Len ->
    length(Main) >= Len ->
        % Iterate through all possible starting points in Main
        lists:any(fun(Start ->
            lists:equal(Sub, lists:sublist(Len, Main, Start))
        end), lists:seq(0, length(Main) - Len))
    ;
        false
    end.

% Helper function to extract a sublist.
lists:sublist(Len, List, Start) ->
    lists:nth(Start, List, Len).

% Helper function to generate sequence of numbers.
lists:seq(Start, End) ->
    lists:seq_internal(Start, End, []).

lists:seq_internal(Current, End, Acc) when Current <= End ->
    lists:seq_internal(Current + 1, End, [Current | Acc]);
lists:seq_internal(_, _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc).

% Main solver function
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),
    SumOfK = 0,

    % Find all divisors k of N
    Divisors = lists:find_divisors(N),

    % Check each divisor
    SumOfK = lists:foldl(fun(K, Acc) ->
        if check_k(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->
            Acc + K
        ;
            Acc
        end
    end, 0, Divisors).

% Function to find all divisors of N
lists:find_divisors(N) ->
    Divs = [],
    % Iterate up to sqrt(N)
    Divs = lists:foldl(fun(K, Acc) ->
        if K * K <= N ->
            if N rem K == 0 ->
                let Divs = [K | Acc],
                if K * K =:= N ->
                    Divs
                else
                    Divs = [N // K | Divs],
                    Divs
                end
            else
                Acc
            end
        else
            Acc
        end, [], lists:seq(1, floor(sqrt(N)))).

% Function to check if a divisor K is a "sortable integer"
check_k(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->
    NumBlocks = N / K,
    AllBlocksSortable = 
        lists:foldl(fun(i, Status) ->
            if Status =:= false -> false
            else
                Start =:= i * K,
                End =:= Start + K,
                
                % Extract block and target block
                Block =:= lists:sublist(K, Nums, Start),
                TargetBlock =:= lists:sublist(K, SortedNums, Start),
                
                % Check cyclic shift property
                if is_cyclic_shift(Block, TargetBlock) ->
                    true
                ;
                    false
                end
            end
        end, true, lists:seq(0, NumBlocks - 1)).

% Assumes both lists have the same length and contain the same elements (multiset equality).
is_cyclic_shift(ListA, ListB) ->
    case {ListA, ListB} of
        {[], []} -> true;
        _ ->
            case get_length(ListA) of
                Length when Length > 0 ->
                    % Concatenate ListB with itself
                    DoubleListB = ListB ++ ListB,
                    % Check if ListA is a sublist of DoubleListB of length Length
                    sublist_of(Length, ListA, DoubleListB)
                _ -> false
            end
    end.

% Helper function to check if 'Sub' is a sublist of 'Main' of a specific length 'Len'.
sublist_of(Len, Sub, Main) when len(Sub) =:= Len ->
    length(Main) >= Len ->
        % Iterate through all possible starting points in Main
        lists:any(fun(Start ->
            lists:equal(Sub, lists:sublist(Len, Main, Start))
        end), lists:seq(0, length(Main) - Len))
    ;
        false
    end.

% Helper function to extract a sublist.
lists:sublist(Len, List, Start) ->
    lists:nth(Start, List, Len).

% Helper function to generate sequence of numbers.
lists:seq(Start, End) ->
    lists:seq_internal(Start, End, []).

lists:seq_internal(Current, End, Acc) when Current <= End ->
    lists:seq_internal(Current + 1, End, [Current | Acc]);
lists:seq_internal(_, _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc).

% Main solver function
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),
    SumOfK = 0,

    % Find all divisors k of N
    Divisors = lists:find_divisors(N),

    % Check each divisor
    SumOfK = lists:foldl(fun(K, Acc) ->
        if check_k(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->
            Acc + K
        ;
            Acc
        end
    end, 0, Divisors).

% Function to find all divisors of N
lists:find_divisors(N) ->
    Divs = [],
    % Iterate up to sqrt(N)
    Divs = lists:foldl(fun(K, Acc) ->
        if K * K <= N ->
            if N rem K == 0 ->
                let Divs = [K | Acc],
                if K * K =:= N ->
                    Divs
                else
                    Divs = [N // K | Divs],
                    Divs
                end
            else
                Acc
            end
        else
            Acc
        end, [], lists:seq(1, floor(sqrt(N)))).

% Function to check if a divisor K is a "sortable integer"
check_k(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->
    NumBlocks = N / K,
    AllBlocksSortable = 
        lists:foldl(fun(i, Status) ->
            if Status =:= false -> false
            else
                Start =:= i * K,
                End =:= Start + K,
                
                % Extract block and target block
                Block =:= lists:sublist(K, Nums, Start),
                TargetBlock =:= lists:sublist(K, SortedNums, Start),
                
                % Check cyclic shift property
                if is_cyclic_shift(Block, TargetBlock) ->
                    true
                ;
                    false
                end
            end
        end, true, lists:seq(0, NumBlocks - 1)).

-module(solver).
-export([solve/1]).

solve(nums) ->
    N = length(nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(nums),
    SumOfK = 0,
    
    % Find divisors and check them
    Divisors = find_divisors(N),
    
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, K) ->
        if check_k(nums, SortedNums, N, K) ->
            Acc + K;
        true ->
            Acc
    end, 0, Divisors).

find_divisors(N) ->
    Divs = [];
    
    fun(D, Acc) ->
        if D * D > N -> Acc;
        true ->
            (
                (N rem D == 0) ->
                    (
                        Acc1 = [D | Acc],
                        (N rem (N div D) == 0) ->
                            Acc2 = [N div D | Acc1],
                            fun(D2, Acc3) ->
                                if D2 > N/D2 -> Acc3;
                                true ->
                                    (
                                        (N rem D2 == 0) ->
                                            Acc4 = [D2 | Acc3];
                                        true ->
                                            Acc4 = Acc3
                                    );
                                    fun(D3, Acc5) ->
                                        if D3 * D3 > N -> Acc5;
                                        true ->
                                            (
                                                (N rem D3 == 0) ->
                                                    [D3 | Acc5];
                                                Acc5
                                            )
                                    end
                            )
                        true
                    )
                true
            )
    end(1, []).

% A more standard way to get divisors in Erlang, though efficiency might be lower for large N
find_divisors(N) ->
    lists:flatten([D || D <- lists:seq(1, floor(sqrt(N))), N rem D == 0, 
                     (D /= N/D) -> [D, N/D]; 
                     true -> [D]]).

% Helper to check if arr is a cyclic shift of target
is_cyclic_shift(Arr, Target) ->
    Len = length(Arr),
    if Len == 0 -> true;
    true ->
        DoubleTarget = Target ++ Target,
        
        fun(Start, Found) ->
            if Start >= Len -> Found;
            true ->
                Match = lists:sublist(DoubleTarget, Start, Len),
                if Match == Arr ->
                    true;
                true ->
                    fun(Start+1, Found) ->
                        if Found -> true;
                        true ->
                            fun(Start+1, Found) ->
                                if Found -> true;
                                true ->
                                    fun(Start+2, Found) ->
                                        if Found -> true;
                                        true ->
                                            fun(Start+2, Found) ->
                                                if Found -> true;
                                                true ->
                                                    (fun(Start, Found) -> 
                                                        if Found -> true;
                                                        false 
                                                    )
                                            end
                                    end
                            end
                    end
        end(0, false).

    Len = length(Arr),

    true ->

        fun(Start, Found) ->

            true ->

                Sublist = lists:sublist(DoubleTarget, Start, Len),
                if Sublist == Arr ->
                    true;
                true ->
                    fun(Start+1, Found) ->
                        if Found -> true;
                        true ->
                            fun(Start+1, Found) ->
                                if Found -> true;
                                true ->
                                    false % Base case if loop finishes without match
                            end
                    end
        end(0, false).


check_k(Nums, SortedNums, N, K) ->
    NumBlocks = N div K,

        if not AllBlocksSortable -> {false, false}; % Optimization: stop early

            IsSortable = is_cyclic_shift(Block, TargetBlock),
            
            if IsSortable ->

            true ->

-module(solve_module).
-export([solve/1]).

%% Helper function to check if list A is a cyclic shift of list B
% Assumes list A and list B have the same length.
is_cyclic_shift(A, B) when length(A) == length(B) ->
    case length(A) of
        0 -> true;
        L -> 
            % Create a doubled list B
            DoubledB = [B | B],
            % Check if A is a contiguous sublist of DoubledB (of length L)
            is_sublist(A, DoubledB, L)
    end.

% Recursive helper to check if sublist A is contained in list B starting at index 0
is_sublist([], _, _) -> true;
is_sublist([A_H | A_T], [_B_H | B_T], L) when L > 0 ->
    case is_sublist(A, B_T, L - 1) of
        true -> true;
        false -> is_sublist([A_H | A_T], B_T, L - 1)
    end;
is_sublist(_A, [], _L) -> false.


%% Check if a given divisor k allows sorting the array nums.
check_k(nums, n, k, sorted_nums) ->
    NumBlocks = n div k,
    
    % Iterate through all blocks
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, i) ->
        if Acc =:= false -> false;
        true ->
            Start = i * k,
            End = Start + k,
            
            % Extract block from nums
            Block = lists:sublist(nums, Start, k),
            
            % Extract target block from sorted_nums
            TargetBlock = lists:sublist(sorted_nums, Start, k),
            
            % Check if the block can be cyclically shifted to match its sorted target
            is_cyclic_shift(Block, TargetBlock)
        end
    end, true, 1..NumBlocks).


%% Main function
solve(nums) ->
    n = length(nums),
    
    % Sort the original array to get the target state
    SortedNums = lists:sort(nums),
    
    SumOfK = 0,
    
    % Find all divisors k of n
    Divisors = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, k) ->
        if Mod(n, k) == 0 ->
            lists:append(Acc, [k])
        else
            Acc
        end
    end, [], 1..n),

    % Filter and sum the valid k's
    SumOfK = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, k) ->
        if check_k(nums, n, k, SortedNums) ->
            Acc + k
        else
            Acc
        end
    end, 0, Divisors).
